article_title: Apple Adds Bosch, Cirrus Logic, TDK, and Qnity to Its American Manufacturing Program With $400 Million Through 2030

content_preview.title: Apple Adds Bosch, Cirrus Logic, TDK, and Qnity to Its American Manufacturing Program With $400 Million Through 2030
content_preview.summary: Apple expands its U.S. supply chain initiative with four new semiconductor and sensor partners, committing $400 million as part of a broader $600 billion domestic investment push.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Apple announced on March 26 that it is adding four new partners to its American Manufacturing Program (AMP): Bosch, Cirrus Logic, TDK, and Qnity Electronics. The company plans to spend $400 million with these partners through 2030, according to the [Apple Newsroom](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-adds-new-partners-to-its-american-manufacturing-program/). The expansion is part of Apple's broader $600 billion, four-year commitment to U.S. manufacturing and innovation.

editor_notes.source_verification: Apple Newsroom confirmed all claims: four partners, $400M through 2030, $600B commitment, Tim Cook quote, TDK TMR sensors, Bosch/TSMC, Qnity/HD MicroSystems, Mac mini Houston, AMP roster. 9to5Mac and CNBC supplementary.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified against Apple primary source.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Good 'What We Don't Know' on spending breakdown and political context.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Apple AMP expansion coverage. Novel topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid article verified from primary Apple Newsroom source. Ready for publication.
